verdict.label: YELLOW · Half size, A-grade only
verdict.desc: The chip rout that started midweek is still dragging the Nasdaq lower, now compounded by a Netflix guidance whiff — NFLX is down over 10% premarket after forecasting slowing sales. The U.S.–Iran conflict is still live in its sixth night of strikes, oil is up more than 11% on the week near one-month highs, and Trump has threatened to hit Iran’s infrastructure next week. But two cooler inflation prints earlier in the week (soft CPI, a rare PPI drop) keep a lid on panic. Composite eased to 48 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) is 33% — just under the 35% trigger. With a live geopolitical tail plus an unresolved chip selloff, GREEN is off the table: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and each name’s print.

composite.band_note: MILD · down from 52 · chip rout persists, Iran still live

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Mon Open)
gap.band_note: just under the 35% line · but chip rout + Iran lock out GREEN

focus.jbig: Chip rout deepens · Netflix whiff · Iran still live · half size
focus.jtag: Semiconductor selloff drags markets to weekly losses while Netflix guidance disappoints — oil up 11% on the week
focus.jnote: Three crosscurrents define the morning. First, the semiconductor selloff keeps deepening: Nasdaq-100 futures shed around 1.6% after the underlying gauge dropped 1.6% Thursday, with the SOX chip index down ~4%; Micron and AMD fell more than 5% each Thursday, SanDisk over 12%, Broadcom about 5%, and US-listed SK Hynix plunged more than 13% as investors question whether AI valuations justify the spending. Second, a Netflix whiff: NFLX fell over 10% premarket after forecasting Q3 revenue of $12.86B and EPS of 82 cents, below the Street’s ~$13B and 84 cents. Third, the Iran conflict is still live — U.S. Central Command said it completed its sixth consecutive night of strikes, oil is up more than 11% on the week, and Trump warned he could target Iran’s infrastructure next week. Softer CPI and a rare PPI drop are the offsetting positive. This is a two-sided, headline-heavy tape — deploy carefully, not aggressively.
focus.notdown: Financials, industrials, and energy are the rotation beneficiaries as money leaves chips — and energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) catches a genuine bid from the oil supply fear. But energy is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper: one de-escalation headline gaps it back down. Watch-only, and any spread needs the 9:35 validation to hold. Half size into a still-live Iran backdrop.
focus.notup: Semiconductors (SMH, MU, AMD, AVGO, SanDisk, chip complex) are the day’s clear pressure point, and Netflix is the fresh single-name gap-down. But selling bear calls into a violent, gapping chip selloff carries brutal snap-back risk on any stabilization headline — and the group has whipsawed all week. The disciplined answer is to wait, not chase the short.
focus.why: Why YELLOW? Composite eased to 48 (MILD) and P(≥1% gap) at 33% sits just under the 35% trigger — both would allow GREEN on their own. What locks GREEN out is the combination: a live, open-ended U.S.–Iran military conflict with oil up 11% on the week plus a violent, still-unfolding memory-chip rout now joined by a Netflix guidance gap. Neither is a scheduled, sizeable-around event. The play: half size, A-grade only, keep DTE clear of the conflict window and each name’s earnings print, and validate at 9:35 before deploying.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~16.5–17, roughly flat; day’s range 15.77–17.23 Thursday, still contained despite the chip rout
Big events (next 1–3 days) — chip rout, NFLX guidance gap, TRV/TFC/FITB today; big-cap tech (GOOGL, TSLA) & more next week
World risk — live U.S.–Iran conflict, sixth straight night of strikes, naval blockade, Trump threatens Iran’s infrastructure
Volatility trend — front-month VIX near 3-month; curve roughly normal but sensitive to any chip/Iran shock
Commodities — oil up 11% on the week ~$79.7, best week since April on Hormuz risk; the biggest swing factor

snapshot.delta: near 2-mo high · oil lifts rate-hike bets
below $4,000 · −3% on the week, near Nov low

setups.read: The oil supply shock is real, durable news — crude is up more than 11% on the week and energy is a clear rotation bid. But this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation headline (Trump has said Tehran signaled willingness to negotiate) and crude gives back $5–7 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way. No earnings issue, but the catalyst itself is the gap risk. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a YELLOW day, don’t chase a war premium. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Money is rotating out of chips into financials, industrials, and energy, and last week’s bank prints (JPM, GS, BAC, WFC, MS) were broadly solid, reinforcing the bid. But this is a broad rotation, not a single-name news gap — no clean per-stock catalyst, and the driver is a risk-off shuffle that can reverse the moment chips stabilize. Watch-only; trade a named catalyst, not the flow.
setups.check_note: A rotation isn’t a catalyst. Don’t underwrite sector drift on a headline-heavy tape.

setups.read: The fundamental lean is down — Micron and AMD fell 5%+ each Thursday, SanDisk over 12%, Broadcom ~5%, and SK Hynix plunged 13%, with the SOX off ~4%. But two problems: a violent selloff like this snaps back hard on any stabilization headline, and the group has whipsawed all week even on strong TSMC results. Selling bear calls into a fast-moving, gapping decline is exactly the squeeze risk 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: Fast, gapping selloff = snap-back risk. Don’t chase the short; wait for the tape to settle.

setups.read: NFLX reported Thursday after the close and gapped down over 10% on soft Q3 guidance ($12.86B rev / 82¢ EPS vs ~$13B / 84¢ est) plus a plan to cut viewing-hours disclosure — a genuine miss-and-guide-down, so earnings risk is behind it (Type A bear-call candidate). But this is a huge single-day gap into a jumpy, chip-pressured tape; big gap-down days can bounce violently intraday. Grade C until the 9:35 validation confirms it’s holding in the lower third of its range.
setups.check_note: Pre-9:35. Only a bear call if NFLX stays below prior close & open, lower third of range at 9:35; half size, short call well above the gap-day high.

setups.read: A chip-rout, risk-off morning is a poor backdrop for clean breakouts. Some oil refiners hit all-time highs earlier in the week on wide crack spreads, but those are now outside their 4-session windows and are war-premium moves, not calm-tape breakouts. No fresh high-volume ATH breakout is holding into today’s open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot into a chip-selloff open — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Split, headline-driven chop
scenarios.sub: Nasdaq pressured by chips + NFLX, Dow/financials/energy held up by rotation; range-bound, vol stays moderate

scenarios.label: Bad case — Chip rout deepens / Iran escalates
scenarios.sub: AI-spend fear spreads, or Trump strikes Iran’s infrastructure; oil runs, equities gap down, VIX pops

scenarios.label: Good case — Chip stabilization + de-escalation
scenarios.sub: The memory rout finds a floor and an Iran backchannel headline eases oil; a relief drift higher, VIX eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure / wider war
scenarios.sub: A strait or Bab el-Mandeb shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: Iran / Strait of Hormuz — the U.S. completed its sixth straight night of strikes; Iran hit U.S. bases in Kuwait and Jordan and Trump threatened Iran’s infrastructure next week. Tanker traffic has fallen to two-month lows — any wider strike sends crude and jet fuel vertical, the week’s biggest tail.

Semiconductor rout — Micron, AMD, SanDisk, Broadcom and SK Hynix all sold off hard Thursday as investors question AI valuations. If the memory selloff keeps spreading, it drags the whole index — the number-one thing to watch today.

Oil near one-month highs & the 10-year — WTI ~$79.7 (up 11% on the week) keeps inflation risk alive even after cooler CPI/PPI; the 10Y is near a two-month high ~4.57–4.60% and markets price ~51% odds of a September Fed hike.
Netflix gap & the earnings cluster — NFLX is down 10%+ on soft guidance; single-stock gaps stacked on macro risk. Keep spreads clear of each name’s print.
Bab el-Mandeb / Red Sea risk — Iran reportedly told the Houthis to be ready to close the Red Sea oil route if its infrastructure is hit. A second chokepoint would compound the Hormuz supply fear.

earnings.intro: Q2 season is broadening — Netflix whiffed after Thursday’s close and mega-cap tech (Alphabet, Tesla, IBM) reports the week of July 21–22, with more financials sprinkled through. With chips selling off and the Iran backdrop still live, a single-stock earnings gap on top of macro risk is a double catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print.

rows.why: Insurer and regional banks round out the financials wave. Last week’s big-bank prints were broadly solid, and the rotation into financials is a supportive backdrop — but each is a single-stock gap risk on its own number.
rows.angle: Don’t hold a spread through any of these names’ print. A clean beat-and-gap can set up a Type A next week.

rows.why: Q3 guidance of $12.86B rev and 82¢ EPS came in below the Street’s ~$13B / 84¢, and Netflix said it would cut viewing-hours disclosure — the stock fell over 10%. Earnings risk is now behind it (Type A), but the gap is large into a jumpy tape.
rows.angle: A miss-and-gap-down is a potential Type A bear-call — but only if it holds the lower third of its range at 9:35. Half size.

rows.why: The first mega-cap prints land into an already-jumpy chip tape — any capex or AI-spend surprise could reignite the selloff. RTX and Lockheed (defense) also report the week of 7/21–23. Stacking single-stock risk on a still-live Iran backdrop.
rows.angle: Keep any tech/defense spread to a DTE that clears the earnings date — and mind the still-live geopolitical tail.

earnings.outro: Fri 7/17: the chip rout and Netflix’s guidance whiff headline the day; Travelers, Truist and Fifth Third report pre-open, with housing starts, industrial production and preliminary Michigan sentiment on the data calendar. It gets busy the week of July 21–22 with mega-cap tech (Alphabet, Tesla, IBM) — against a still-live Iran backdrop. Half size, A-grade only, until the tape settles.
